name: Span Calculator
summary: Span Calculator computes the span and exposure margin for a basket of derivative positions. You POST a jData JSON object containing an account id and an array of position objects (each describing exchange/instrument/symbol/expiry/option-type/strike and buy/sell/net quantities); the response returns span and exposure margin values, plus variants that ignore the buyqty/sellqty open quantities.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list (actid, pos[]).

json_fields.name: actid
json_fields.description: Any Account id, preferably actual account id if sending from post login screen.

json_fields.name: pos
json_fields.description: Array of json objects. (object fields given in below table.) Each object describes one position; object fields are exch, instname, symname, expd, optt, strprc, buyqty, sellqty, netqty.

json_fields.name: exch
json_fields.description: Exchange (field of each object in the pos array).

json_fields.name: instname
json_fields.description: Instrument name (field of each object in the pos array).

json_fields.name: symname
json_fields.description: Symbol name (field of each object in the pos array).

json_fields.name: expd
json_fields.description: Expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format (field of each object in the pos array).

json_fields.name: optt
json_fields.description: Option Type (field of each object in the pos array).

json_fields.name: strprc
json_fields.description: Strike price (field of each object in the pos array).

json_fields.name: buyqty
json_fields.description: Buy Open Quantity (field of each object in the pos array).

json_fields.name: sellqty
json_fields.description: Sell Open Quantity (field of each object in the pos array).

json_fields.name: netqty
json_fields.description: Net traded quantity (field of each object in the pos array).

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: Market watch success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: span_trade
response_fields.description: Span value ignoring input fields buyqty, sellqty.
response_fields.possible_values: Span value ignoring input fields buyqty, sellqty

response_fields.name: expo_trade
response_fields.description: Exposure margin ignoring input fields buyqty, sellqty.
response_fields.possible_values: Exposure margin ignoring input fields buyqty, sellqty

notes: The documented URL is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI/SpanCalc; the sample curl uses a placeholder host 'https://BaseURL/SpanCalc'. The request is sent as a form-encoded body 'jData={...}&jKey=...' with Content-Type: application/json (per the doc's curl). jKey is the session/API key appended to the request body in the sample but is NOT a documented query parameter (the only documented query parameter is jData*). actid and pos are the only asterisked (mandatory) jData fields; the per-position object sub-fields (exch, instname, symname, expd, optt, strprc, buyqty, sellqty, netqty) carry no asterisk. The section spans two pages: title, URL, query params, actid/pos, and the first pos sub-field (exch) are on page 58; the remaining pos sub-fields and the response details are on page 59. No dedicated sample success/failure JSON response is provided for SpanCalc; the JSON visible in the page-58 right column ({"stat":"Ok","norentm":"02-05-1975 08:48:52","msgtyp":"Admin Message","dmsg":"Test Msg All Message Recovery2"}) belongs to the preceding section and was excluded.
issues: No sample success/failure response JSON exists for SpanCalc in the source, so those fields are empty. The draft's invented 'jKey' query parameter was dropped (jKey is only a body-appended auth token, not a documented query-parameter row in the image). All json_fields, response_fields, and the verbatim curl were confirmed against the page-58/59 images.
